revision.text:
[[ja:赤堀茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Akabori Chausuyama Kofun (赤堀茶臼山古墳) |image = File:Akabori Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg |alternate_name = Akabori Village No. 260 Mound |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|qid=Q328596|lt=Isesaki|1=Isesaki}}, Akabori Imaicho 2-chome Ko 995-1 and others (Azabukushima) |coordinates = {{Coord|36.387|N|139.207528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 62.4m
Height: 6m (Rear circular mound) |burial = 2 charcoal chambers |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |AccompanyingTumuli = |built = Mid-5th century |InterredPerson = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Designated as an Isesaki City Historic Site ("Akabori Chausuyama Kofun") |TangibleHistoric Site = |Specialnotes = |map = Japan Gunma |type = Scallop-shaped kofun-West |map_dot_label = Akabori Chausuyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Akabori Chausuyama Kofun''' (赤堀茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Akabori Imaicho, {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|qid=Q328596|lt=Isesaki|1=Isesaki}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. The kofun is scallop-shaped and has been Historic Site1 as an Isesaki City Historic Site. It is well-known for the discovery of numerous {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, including eight house-shaped haniwa. == Overview == The kofun was constructed atop the eastern ridge of the Tadazan Hills in eastern Gunma Prefecture. Excavations were carried out by the Imperial Household Museum in 1929 (Shōwa 4), and subsequent surveys for area confirmation took place from 1995 (Heisei 7) onward. The mound has a front-and-rear keyhole type with a short front section, classified as a scallop-shaped kofun.The classification has been debated, with some suggesting it is a short-front keyhole-shaped kofun based on later surveys (Toyo Maehara, ''The Influence of the Eastern Nobility: The Ōmuro Kofun Cluster [Gunma]'', Shinsensha, 2009, pp. 86–88). This article adheres to the description in the Isesaki City pamphlet. The mound's main axis runs perpendicular to the ridge, with the front section facing west. The mound was constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun (Supplementary Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002|qq=}} On the mound's exterior, cylindrical haniwa rows (including morning glory- and jar-shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (including eight house-shaped haniwa, fenced types, short armor types, grass skirt types, lid types, chair types, high stand types, and rooster types) have been identified.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} Additionally, stone paving with fractured and river stones was observed.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} A surrounding moat is present around the mound.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} The burial facility consists of two charcoal chambers at the apex of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} burial goods discovered include a six-figure mirror, swords, spears, axes, and iron arrowheads from the first chamber, while the second chamber yielded a floral-patterned mirror and a large sword. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-5th century of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} This is also one of the earliest academically surveyed kofun in Gunma Prefecture, and its burial goods are significant for understanding aristocratic mansions and haniwa arrangements of the period. The kofun area was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Akabori Town in 2004 (now Isesaki City). == Excavation History == * 1929 (Shōwa 4): Excavation by the Imperial Household Museum; report published in 1933. * 1995–1997 (Heisei 7–9): Area confirmation surveys by Akabori Town Board of Education; summary reports published in 1996, 1997, and 1998. * 2004 (Heisei 16): Designated a historic site by Akabori Town on August 10 (later transferred to Isesaki City). == Mound == [[File:Akabori Chausuyama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Top of the Rear Circular Mound|qq=}}]] [[File:Akabori Chausuyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the Rear Circular Mound to the Front Section|qq=}}{{small|In the background: {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.|qq=}}]] Mound area are as follows:{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} * Total length: 62.4 meters * Rear circular mound: ''' Diameter: 50.2 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * Front section: ''' Length: 18 meters ''' Height: 4 meters The moat is continuous along the north, west, and south sides but absent on the rear mound's eastern side, likely due to the slope of the terrain.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} == burial goods == === burial goods === burial goods recovered from the burial chambers by the Imperial Household Museum's excavation include: * First chamber: ** Six-figure mirror ** Swords, spears, and axes ** Iron arrowheads ** Triangular leather armor and helmets ** Stone imitations * Second chamber: ** Floral-patterned mirror ** Large sword === Haniwa === [[File:赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 家形埴輪群.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center| part_of of House-shaped Haniwa|qq=}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} (same for other images).|qq=}}]] burial goods from the Imperial Household Museum's survey include: * Eight house-shaped haniwa: ** Three gabled houses (one main house with wooden fish-shaped decorations, two auxiliary with reed screens) ** Four warehouse haniwa (three gabled, one hip-roofed) ** One shed * Figurative haniwa, including: ** Fenced types ** Short armor types ** Grass skirt types ** Lid types ** Rooster types (identified as a pair with another from the same production area) Additional surveys by Akabori Town revealed various types, including swords, shields, helmets, cylindrical haniwa, and others.{{Sfn|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun Pamphlet|qq=}} 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 切妻造家 (J-2dd40).JPG|Gabled house 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 切妻造家 (J-2dd41).JPG|Gabled house 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 切妻造倉庫 (J-2dd45).JPG|Gabled warehouse 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 切妻造倉庫 (J-2dd44).JPG|Gabled warehouse 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 寄棟造倉庫 (J-2dd43).JPG|Hipped-roof warehouse 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 切妻造家 (J-2dd42).JPG|Shed 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped haniwa 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 埴輪椅子.JPG|Chair-shaped haniwa 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 高坏形埴輪.JPG|Pedestal-shaped haniwa 赤堀茶臼山古墳出土 鶏形埴輪 (J-2dd48).JPG|Chicken-shaped haniwa 赤堀茶臼山古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the Akabori History and Folklore Museum, Isesaki City (the gabled house haniwa is a model).|qq=}} == Historic Site == === Isesaki City Designated Cultural Property === * Historic Site: ''' Akabori Chausuyama Kofun - Designated on August 10, 2004.[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/2570.html Akabori Chausuyama Kofun] - Isesaki City Homepage.Site Intypeation Board. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} ({{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, Tokyo) - Preserves burial goods from the Imperial Household Museum's survey. * Akabori Historical Folklore Museum (Isesaki City, Nishikubo Town) - Preserves burial goods from Akabori Town Board of Education's survey. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/hogo/bunkazaihogo/shitei/shiseki/2570.html Akabori Chausuyama Kofun] - Isesaki City Homepage {{translated page|ja|赤堀茶臼山古墳|version=104508026|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:赤堀茶臼山古墳]] {{Commons category|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17191031}} [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gunma Prefecture]]
